result.markdown:
--- title: "[cv_coxnet()]{.doc-object-name .doc-function .doc-label .doc-label-function}" body-classes: doc-api-page page-navigation: false html-table-processing: none --- ::: {.doc-subject} Select the `CoxNet` penalizer by k-fold cross-validation. ::: ::: {.doc-usage-source} Usage [Source](https://github.com/rich-iannone/greenwood/blob/main/greenwood/_penalized.py#L653-L932){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} ::: ::: {.doc-signature .doc-Kind.FUNCTION} ```python cv_coxnet( surv, covariates, *, data=None, l1_ratio=1.0, penalizers=None, n_penalizers=50, eps=0.001, k=5, metric="concordance", times=None, standardize=True, max_iter=1000, tol=1e-07, stratified=True, seed=None ) ``` ::: ::: {.doc-text} Evaluates a log-spaced grid of penalizer values (or a user-supplied sequence) with stratified k-fold cross-validation and returns the value that maximises concordance (or minimises the integrated Brier score). ::: ## Parameters {.doc-parameters} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [surv]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Surv]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : A right-censored or counting-process `Surv` response. [covariates]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Any]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : Covariate design (dataframe, 2-D array, or formula string with `data`). [data]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Any]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [None]{.doc-parameter-default} : DataFrame to evaluate a formula `covariates` string against. [l1_ratio]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [[1.0]{.fl}]{.doc-parameter-default} : Elastic-net mixing parameter (default `1.0` = lasso). Fixed across the entire path (only the `penalizer` is varied). [penalizers]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Any]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [None]{.doc-parameter-default} : An explicit sequence of penalizer values to evaluate. When `None` (the default) a log-spaced path of `n_penalizers=` values is generated automatically from `lambda_max` down to `eps * lambda_max`. [n_penalizers]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [int]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [[50]{.dv}]{.doc-parameter-default} : Number of log-spaced values in the auto-generated path (the default is `50`). Ignored when `penalizers=` is supplied. [eps]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [[0.001]{.fl}]{.doc-parameter-default} : Ratio of smallest to largest auto-generated penalizer (default of `1e-3`). Smaller values extend the path toward weaker regularisation. Ignored when `penalizers=` is supplied. [k]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [int]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [[5]{.dv}]{.doc-parameter-default} : Number of cross-validation folds (the default is `5`). [metric]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [str]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [["concordance"]{.st}]{.doc-parameter-default} : Scoring metric: - `"concordance"` (the default): Harrell's C-statistic, where higher is better. - `"brier"`: integrated IPCW Brier score, which requires `times=` (here lower is better). [times]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Any]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [None]{.doc-parameter-default} : Evaluation times for `metric="brier"` (1-D array-like, $\ge 2$ values). [standardize]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [bool]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [[True]{.va}]{.doc-parameter-default} : Standardize covariates before penalising (default is `True`). Must match the `standardize=` argument used for the final `CoxNet` fit. [max_iter]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [int]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [[1000]{.dv}]{.doc-parameter-default} : FISTA iteration limit and convergence tolerance for each inner fit. [tol]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [int]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [[1000]{.dv}]{.doc-parameter-default} : FISTA iteration limit and convergence tolerance for each inner fit. [stratified]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [bool]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [[True]{.va}]{.doc-parameter-default} : Use stratified k-fold to preserve the event rate in every fold (the default is `True`). [seed]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [int | None]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [None]{.doc-parameter-default} : Random seed for reproducible fold assignments. ::: ## Returns {.doc-returns} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [CoxNetCVResult]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : Object exposing `best_penalizer_`, `penalizer_1se_`, `mean_scores_`, `std_scores_`, `n_nonzero_`, and a `to_frame()` method for the path. ::: ## Details {.doc-details} **Lambda path.** When `penalizers=` is not supplied the path is $$ \lambda_{\max} = \frac{\max_j \bigl|\nabla_j\,\ell(\beta=0)\bigr|}{n \cdot \max(\alpha,\, 0.01)}, \quad \lambda_i = \lambda_{\max} \cdot \varepsilon^{i/(m-1)}, \quad i = 0,\ldots,m-1 $$ where $\nabla\ell(\beta=0)$ is the score of the partial log-likelihood at the null model, $\alpha$ = `l1_ratio`, $\varepsilon$ = `eps`, and $m$ = `n_penalizers`. **1-SE rule.** `penalizer_1se_` is the *largest* penalizer (most regularized) whose mean score lies within one standard error of `best_score_`. Prefer it when parsimony matters as it yields a sparser model at no significant loss of predictive accuracy. **Fitting the final model.** After selecting a penalizer, refit on all data: ```python result = gw.cv_coxnet(y, x, seed=23) final = gw.CoxNet(penalizer=result.best_penalizer_).fit(y, x) ``` ## Examples {.doc-examples} Run cross-validated lasso selection on the `lung` dataset: ::: {#b0c1be1f .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import greenwood as gw lung = gw.load_dataset("lung", backend="polars") y = gw.Surv.right(lung["time"], event=(lung["status"] == 2)) cols = ["age", "sex", "ph.ecog", "ph.karno", "wt.loss"] result = gw.cv_coxnet(y, lung[cols], l1_ratio=1.0, n_penalizers=10, k=3, seed=23) result ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ``` CoxNetCV (concordance, ↑ higher is better, l1_ratio=1.0, 3-fold) best penalizer : 0.046941 (mean concordance: 0.6377) 1-SE penalizer : 0.10113 (mean concordance: 0.6358) 10 penalizers tested, range [0.000218, 0.218] ``` ::: ::: Inspect the full penalizer path: ::: {#b4396366 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} result.to_frame(format="polars") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ```{=html}
shape: (10, 4)
penalizermean_scorestd_scoren_nonzero
f64f64f64f64
0.2178820.516310.028250.333333
0.1011320.6357980.0465192.333333
0.0469410.6377120.0329184.0
0.0217880.6362730.0328884.333333
0.0101130.6329440.0328195.0
0.0046940.6347510.0325185.0
0.0021790.63330.0338555.0
0.0010110.6325270.0346775.0
0.0004690.6325010.0347255.0
0.0002180.6330180.0350035.0
``` ::: ::: Fit the final model at the best penalizer: ::: {#b40c3392 .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} final = gw.CoxNet(penalizer=result.best_penalizer_, l1_ratio=1.0).fit(y, lung[cols]) final ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ``` CoxNet (elastic-net Cox, penalizer=0.046941245091417096, l1_ratio=1.0) coef age 0.006585 sex -0.4198 ph.ecog 0.3761 ph.karno 0 wt.loss -0.001172 n = 213, events = 151, nonzero coefficients = 4 ``` ::: ::: Use the 1-SE rule for a sparser model: ::: {#568ef2e3 .cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} sparse = gw.CoxNet(penalizer=result.penalizer_1se_, l1_ratio=1.0).fit(y, lung[cols]) sparse ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=4} ``` CoxNet (elastic-net Cox, penalizer=0.10113184681823788, l1_ratio=1.0) coef age 0 sex -0.2499 ph.ecog 0.2646 ph.karno -0 wt.loss -0 n = 213, events = 151, nonzero coefficients = 2 ``` ::: :::
